pi_items.source: Task description specifies this exact name for the view; verified against verifier.py regex pattern
Task description: 'Artist (from Artist table)'. Verified by querying actual Chinook database: artists.Name field
Task description: 'Album (from Album table)'. Verified by querying actual Chinook database: albums.Title field
Task description: 'Track (Name from Track table)'. Verified by querying actual Chinook database: tracks.Name field
Task description: 'Duration' - formatted as MM:SS from Milliseconds column
Used to convert milliseconds to minutes: milliseconds/60000 = minutes (since 60000ms = 60 sec = 1 min)
Used to convert remainder milliseconds to seconds: (milliseconds % 60000) / 1000 = seconds
SQL string concatenation operator, used in HSQLDB to join formatted minutes and seconds
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM artists in Chinook database
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM albums in Chinook database
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tracks in Chinook database
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM customers in Chinook database
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM invoices in Chinook database
PRAGMA table_info(tracks) confirms 'Milliseconds' INTEGER column exists in tracks table

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the real Chinook sample database (275 artists, 347 albums, 3503 tracks) to create a formatted view. The view requires displaying Artist, Album, and Track columns with a Duration field formatted as MM:SS from the Milliseconds column using the formula: minutes (milliseconds/60000) and seconds ((milliseconds % 60000)/1000), joined with concatenation operator (||). The specific column names are 'Artist' (from artists.Name), 'Album' (from albums.Title), 'Track' (from tracks.Name), and 'Duration' (formatted).
